raw_consumer_statement:
I purchased airline tickets from XXXX using my Citibank credit card. When I arrived at the airport for my scheduled travel, XXXX staff informed me that the ticket reflected the wrong travel date and refused to allow me to board. 

While still at the airport counter, I attempted to immediately resolve the issue by purchasing another XXXX ticket on my phone. That second purchase again reflected an incorrect date, and XXXX again refused to allow me to board. As a result, I was denied transportation twice on the same day despite making immediate, good-faith attempts to correct the issue at the airport. 

XXXX refused to provide any correction or refund, even though no transportation services were provided. I was forced to purchase a replacement ticket from a different airline in order to complete my travel. 

I disputed the charge with Citibank on the basis that services were not rendered. Citibank initially issued a provisional credit but later denied the dispute and reversed the credit. Citibank relied on generic fare rules provided by the merchant and did not address the denial of boarding, the immediate attempted correction at the airport, or the fact that no transportation services were provided. Citibank also did not provide a substantive, transaction-specific explanation or meaningful disclosure of the evidence relied upon.
